title: Framework Risk Coverage Matrix
description: Which frameworks address which risk domains. Based on explicit provisions, not implied coverage.

discrimination-bias.notes: Principle 1 (Fairness) addresses broadly but lacks specific technical requirements

privacy-surveillance.notes: Principle 3 (Privacy and Security) addresses data protection

safety-reliability.notes: Principle 2 (Reliability, Safety and Control) is comprehensive

autonomy-human-control.notes: Principles 2 and 7 address human control and benefit

security-adversarial.notes: General security mentioned but no adversarial-specific provisions

discrimination-bias.notes: Art. 10 data governance, Art. 5 prohibits biometric categorisation

privacy-surveillance.notes: Art. 5 bans social scoring and mass surveillance, Art. 10 data governance

transparency-opacity.notes: Art. 13, 50, 85 comprehensive transparency and right to explanation

security-adversarial.notes: Art. 15 cybersecurity, Art. 55 adversarial testing for systemic risk GPAI

accountability-gaps.notes: Art. 16-26 detailed provider/deployer obligations, Art. 17 QMS

environmental.notes: Art. 55 energy efficiency for systemic risk GPAI only

misinformation.notes: Art. 5 bans manipulation, Art. 50 deepfake labelling

ip-copyright.notes: Art. 53 Copyright Directive compliance for GPAI

security-adversarial.notes: MANAGE-2.3 response to unknown risks, MEASURE-2.7 security

societal-impact.notes: MAP-5 impacts to individuals, groups, communities, society

privacy-surveillance.notes: A.7.4 data privacy, A.8.4 data security

autonomy-human-control.notes: A.3.2 roles and responsibilities for oversight

accountability-gaps.notes: Clause 9 audit, Clause 10 improvement, A.9.4 logs

societal-impact.notes: A.3.3 impact assessment, A.3.5 stakeholder engagement

coverageLegend.strong: Explicit, detailed provisions addressing this risk domain
coverageLegend.moderate: Provisions address this domain partially or indirectly
